evidence: `src/shared/contexts/ProjectContext.tsx` writes `window.__PROJECT_CONTEXT__` in an effect, creating an implicit global dependency channel outside React/context boundaries.

`src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts` is still an active compatibility path with a documented removal target (`2026-06-30`), but remains a runtime-accessible boundary instead of a compile-time migration fence.

UI boundary usage is inconsistent: `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/LightboxShell.tsx` and `src/shared/components/ui/sonner.tsx` import Base UI primitives directly while `src/pages/Home/components/install/InstallInstructionsModal.tsx` uses shared `ui/dialog` wrappers.

Gallery behavior orchestration is spread across separate modules with large coordination surfaces (`useMediaGalleryHandlers`, `useMobileInteractions`, `ShotActions`) that pass many callbacks/refs/state handles rather than a single cohesive controller interface.

`src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/components/ShotActions.tsx` contains navigation, async action dispatch, quick-create wiring, tooltip UX, and selector control in one component-level contract, increasing ownership ambiguity.
`src/pages/Home/components/panes/CreativePartnerPane.tsx` mixes heavy marketing content, embedded iframe behavior, and tooltip interaction policy in one pane module, reducing top-level role clarity.

findings.summary: ProjectContext exports state through a window global, bypassing module boundaries
evidence: `ProjectContext` assigns `window.__PROJECT_CONTEXT__ = { selectedProjectId, projects }` on every relevant state change.
The file comment explicitly documents non-context consumers relying on this fallback, making dependency direction implicit and non-typed.
This introduces hidden cross-module coupling and out-of-band state reads that are not visible in import contracts.
findings.suggestion: Replace `window.__PROJECT_CONTEXT__` with an explicit access layer (for example, a small project-selection store module with typed subscribe/get APIs) and migrate callers to that API; then remove the global write path.

findings.summary: Legacy Supabase facade remains an active architectural seam past migration phase
evidence: `legacySupabaseFacade.ts` still exports `getLegacySupabaseClient()` and is designed as a compatibility access path.
The deprecation metadata includes a concrete removal target (`2026-06-30`) but the facade is still callable and part of runtime dependency flow.
Keeping live legacy accessors in shared runtime paths prolongs dual-boundary architecture and encourages continued coupling to old entry points.
findings.suggestion: Convert legacy facade usage into a hard migration boundary: block new imports via lint rule/path ban, add runtime assertion in non-test builds, and migrate remaining call sites to `getSupabaseClient()` before removal date.

findings.summary: UI modules bypass shared wrappers inconsistently, fragmenting component boundaries
evidence: `LightboxShell` imports `@base-ui-components/react/dialog` directly.
`sonner.tsx` imports `@base-ui-components/react/toast` directly and defines separate toast plumbing.
Other app modules in scope (for example `InstallInstructionsModal`) consume shared `ui/dialog` abstractions, so boundary usage is mixed for similar UI concerns.
findings.suggestion: Standardize one UI boundary policy: either route all app code through shared `ui/*` wrappers or define a strict exception layer; then move direct Base UI imports behind shared primitives to reduce cross-module volatility.

findings.summary: Media gallery ownership is split across coordination-heavy hooks/components
evidence: `useMediaGalleryHandlers` requires a broad argument bag mixing navigation, lightbox lifecycle, modal control, filters, and shot context reactions.
`useMobileInteractions` depends on externally managed timing refs (`lastTouchTimeRef`, `lastTappedImageIdRef`, `doubleTapTimeoutRef`) plus multiple setter callbacks.
`ShotActions` consumes multiple nested state/callback bundles (`selector`, `status`, `quickCreate`, `actions`) and performs both UI rendering and workflow decisions.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single feature-level controller for gallery-item interactions (shot actions, mobile tap semantics, lightbox/task transitions) that exposes a narrower view-model to presentational components; move timing and workflow transitions into that controller.

findings.summary: CreativePartnerPane combines static marketing content with complex tooltip behavior policy
evidence: `CreativePartnerPane` contains long-form marketing copy/structure, external embed iframe, and platform-specific tooltip interaction state transitions in a single component.
The component contract receives multiple tooltip policy controls (`ecosystemTipOpen`, `ecosystemTipDisabled`, setters) in addition to pane navigation concerns.
Compared with `InstallInstructionsModal` (presentation + scenario resolution), this pane has mixed ownership boundaries that make role/purpose less focused.
findings.suggestion: Split `CreativePartnerPane` into: (1) static content sections, (2) a dedicated ecosystem-embed interaction component managing its own open/disable state policy, and (3) pane shell composition; keep the pane API focused on open/close and navigation only.
